notes: Runs great out of the box with DX9 and have a good performance, too. No tweaks needed. DX11 doesn't work and the ingame shop isn't available. I can't use the Legacy Client.
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes:
In addition to my previous report:
It is possible to use the legacy client by adding the environment variable "UseLegacyClient = true" under the paragraph [Launcher] into the UserPreference.Ini. 
Press the launcher menue button, then take the screenshot-folder. There you will find the UserPreference.Ini.

Or you can find the file there:
/home/user/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/212500/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/The Lord of the Rings Online
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes: Everything works perfectly out of the box. Now also the Lotro Shop works out of the box. Don't select the DirectX 11 ("high resolution graphics") option. The game runs great on DirectX 9
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes: Everything works perfectly out of the box. Now also the Lotro Shop works out of the box (if it doesnt work: install winhttp with winetricks). Don't select the DirectX 11 ("high resolution graphics") option. The game runs great on DirectX 9
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes:
Previously made a gold entry after playing the game for 10-15 hours without issue (other than the initial install and lack of dx11) however, I've started to notice that sometimes when you try to access the store or "help" section of the game, the game will minimise to the tray. Maximising the game again will give a blank screen with just the audio continuing. Only option is to "pkill -9 lotro"  and relaunch the game.

Avoiding the store during quests/raids is a sufficient workaround though :) As far as performance goes, the game is now much closer to windows than my previous report suggested.
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes:
Game runs and installs perfectly out of the box, apart from DX10/11 and the LotRO store. 

For enabling DX11, follow this guide at the link below. You'll need to install D3DX11_42, D3DX11_43 and D3DCompiler_43 using protontricks, then manually copy the files for D3DCompiler_42 using the script the author has created. Afterwards, DX11 works flawlessly.

https://www.reddit.com/r/SteamPlay/comments/9umhk2/getting_directx11_working_in_lord_of_the_rings/

In order to get the LotRO store working, you need to go into the options on the launcher and select the 'compatibility client'. After this, the LotRO store will open in an external browser, giving you full access.
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes: The game only runs in DX9 without jumping through a lot of hoops. I've had it running in DX11 using external winetricks tweaks but they aren't consistent and crashes happen much more frequently and ended up breaking altogether.
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes:

I did use protonfixes, so maybe that played a part in it.

title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes: The game is running good with no problems. The store also works! This runs better then World of Warcraft with Linux because wow has issues with stutters and slowdowns at times. (Blizzard had issues with android and linux network traffic not working after the ddoss attack last week but they fixed the issue and classic and retail now work!)
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™

notes: 64-bit client works OOB perfectly. On the DX11 version the Lotro store works perfectly. No artifacts, performance is parity with Windows. No tweaks, no env variables needed, no nothing. Just click "install" then "play" and select the high quality and DX11 versions as prompted. No issues whatsoever.
title: The Lord of the Rings Online™
